content_markdown: A pipe of length $0.6\text{ m}$ is filled with a gas and closed at one end. The speed of sound in the gas is $300\text{ m s}^{-1}$. What are the frequencies of the first two harmonics in the tube?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe closed at one end, the fundamental frequency (first harmonic) is $f_1 = \frac{v}{4L}$. Substituting the values: $f_1 = \frac{300}{4 \times 0.6} = \frac{300}{2.4} = 125\text{ Hz}$. In a closed pipe, only odd harmonics exist ($f_1, 3f_1, 5f_1, \dots$). Therefore, the second harmonic (the next available frequency) is $3f_1 = 3 \times 125 = 375\text{ Hz}$.
